keyPoints: ARIMA(p,d,q) models combine autoregressive (AR), differencing (I), and moving average (MA) components.
The 'd' parameter represents the order of differencing required to make the time series stationary.
ARIMA models are used for forecasting non-seasonal time series data.
commonMistakes: Over-differencing the series, which can introduce unnecessary correlation and harm model performance.
Failing to check for stationarity before and after differencing, leading to spurious results.
